clusters.label: A
clusters.summary: The San Gabriel Mountains are at a high risk of wildfires, with an average fire probability of 75.6% and a range of 58.5%–91.9%. This is due to the area's historical fire-proneness and extreme nighttime temperatures. We are closely monitoring the situation and recommend that residents prepare their defensible spaces and have evacuation plans in place.
actions: Create defensible space, prepare go-bags, know evacuation routes (CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO: READY)
Position Type 1 engines within 15-min response time of high-risk areas
Monitor weather conditions for extreme heat stress and rapid fire spread

clusters.label: B
clusters.summary: The Shasta-Trinity Region is also at a high risk of wildfires, with an average fire probability of 65.0% and a range of 51.7%–79.5%. This is due to the area's historical fire-proneness and extreme nighttime temperatures. We recommend that residents take necessary precautions and be prepared to evacuate if necessary.
actions: Create defensible space, prepare go-bags, know evacuation routes (CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO: READY)
Position Type 1 engines within 15-min response time of high-risk areas
Monitor weather conditions for extreme heat stress and rapid fire spread

clusters.label: C

This is due to the area's historical fire-proneness and extreme nighttime temperatures. We recommend that residents take necessary precautions and be prepared to evacuate if necessary.
actions: Create defensible space, prepare go-bags, know evacuation routes (CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO: READY)
Position Type 1 engines within 15-min response time of high-risk areas
Monitor weather conditions for extreme heat stress and rapid fire spread

overall_assessment: California is experiencing a high risk of wildfires, with 24 zones at VERY_HIGH risk and 81 zones at HIGH risk. Residents in these areas should take necessary precautions and be prepared to evacuate if necessary.
